homepage.text_excerpt: Sir Edward Leigh MP | Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency Skip to main content Sir Edward Leigh MP Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency Main navigation Home News About Sir Edward Leigh About the Constituency Campaign Responses In Parliament Contact Sir Edward Leigh MP Welcome to my website. I am the Conservative Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency. Here you can read my most recent news and find out about my work as an MP, whether in Parliament or in the constituency. I am here to help everyone of the inhabitants of the Gainsborough constituency. I am at your service! pages.title: About the Rt Hon Sir Edward Leigh MP | Sir Edward Leigh MP
pages.meta_description: Sir Edward Leigh is the Conservative Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency in Lincolnshire. He was first elected to represent the constituency in 1983.

pages.text_excerpt: About the Rt Hon Sir Edward Leigh MP | Sir Edward Leigh MP Skip to main content Sir Edward Leigh MP Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency Main navigation Home News About Sir Edward Leigh About the Constituency Campaign Responses In Parliament Contact About the Rt Hon Sir Edward Leigh MP Tweet Sir Edward Leigh is the Conservative Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency in Lincolnshire. He was first elected to represent the constituency in 1983. A former minister, from 2001 to 2010 Sir Edward was Chairman of the Public Accounts Committee — a role he relinquished after serving the maximum term. Born in London in 1950, Edward was educated at St Philip’s School, London, the Oratory School, Berkshire, and the Lycée Français in London. He studied history at Durham University, where he was President of the Durham Union Society. Sir Edward is the younger son of Sir Neville Leigh KCVO, former clerk to the Privy Council. He is married, with three daughters and three sons. Sir Edward was a member of Richmond Borough Council before being elected to the Greater London Council in 1974, serving until 1981. He worked in the private office of Mrs Thatcher in 1976-77 as private secretary in charge of her correspondence as Leader of the Opposition. In the October 1974 General Election, Sir Edward fought Teesside Middlesborough for the Conservative party and was elected Member of Parliament for Gainsborough & Horncastle in July 1983. He served as Joint Secretary of the Conservative Parliamentary Defence Committee and the Parliamentary Agricultural Committee from 1983 to 1985. Sir Edward was Chairman of the National Council for Civil Defence and was Director of the Coalition for Peace Through Security until early 1985. From 1990 until 1993, Sir Edward was a Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (a junior minister) in the Department of Trade & Industry. Prior to that he was a Parliamentary Private Secretary in the Home Office. In May 1997, he was elected Member of Parliament for the redefined seat of Gainsborough with a majority of 6,826. After hovering around 8,000 in the 2001 and 2005 elections, his majority rose to 10,559 in 2010 and again to 15,449 in 2015. Sir Edward's majority continued to rise to 17,023 in 2017. Most recently, in the winter general election of 2019, Sir Edward's majority rose, once again, to 22,967. Sir Edward is a former member of the Social Security Select Committee. Until 2015, he was Chairman of the Conservative Backbench Committee on Foreign Affairs, Defence, and International Relations. He has served as an Independent Financial Advisor to the Treasury and serves on the Treasury’s Financial Reporting & Advisory Board. From 2011 until 2020 he served as Chairman of the Public Accounts Commission, the body which audits the National Audit Office. Sir Edward is a qualified barrister and a member of the Inner Temple, and practised in arbitration and criminal law for Goldsmiths Chambers. He is a Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators. He was knighted by the Queen in 2013, made an officier of the Legion d’honneur by the French government in 2015, and in 2017 was made a commendatore of the Order of the Star of Italy. At the end of 2018 Her Majesty the Queen appointed Sir Edward Leigh MP to the Privy Council. Twitter Please click here to see the latest tweets from Sir Edward Leigh, the MP for Gainsborough. Another Country Edward Leigh also has a private site where he writes from a Christian perspective: Edward Leigh's Another Country . pages.text_excerpt: Campaign Responses | Sir Edward Leigh MP Skip to main content Sir Edward Leigh MP Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency Main navigation Home News About Sir Edward Leigh About the Constituency Campaign Responses In Parliament Contact Campaign Responses Tweet Because of the high volume of mass campaign emails I now receive it is no longer possible for me to respond to each one of them individually, as I would prefer. Instead, responses to campaign emails are posted here, the most recent appearing at the top of the list below. On the lefthand menu you will see listed government departments, responses are grouped onto these pages. If you can't find what you are looking for please try the search function by clicking here . Sometimes it may take a week or two to get a campaign response up, so please check back to see if one has been posted. Constituents who write to me personally will be answered to by letter, as mentioned on the contact page. Game Shooting Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about game shooting.While I appreciate your concerns about game shooting, it brings vital income to rural communities and the land management for game shooting can bring significant benefits to the environment. 'Cats are Family Too' campaign (Kept Animals Bill) Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about cats and the Animal Welfare (Kept Animals) Bill. The United Kingdom has some of the highest animal welfare standards in the world and we should what we can to protect our world-leading standards. Defend Nature (Wildlife Trusts Campaign) Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about planning policy and local wildlife. Wildlife sites are vital to our countryside and our environment and it is a vital duty to protect them and keep them secure for the future. The National Planning Policy Framework lists local wildlife site Royal Mail Customer Service Point Closures Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about Royal Mail and their Customer Service Points. I share your disappointment as I know how vital Royal Mail and the services it provides are for so many communities across the country, particularly in rural areas like ours. In November Royal M Frequent Flyer Levy Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about a Frequent Flyer Levy. As part of a consultation on aviation tax reform between March and June 2021, the Government sought views on whether a frequent flyer levy could replace Air Passenger Duty (APD) as the principal tax on the aviation sector. Minimum Service Laws Dear Constituent, Thank you for your correspondence regarding minimum service laws. Workers’ ability to organise and work collectively is a fundamental part of our free society and of a free market. On-shore Wind Farms Dear Constituent, Thank you for your correspondence regarding on-shore wind farms. This debate seems to have re-opened yet again, but it is only just a few years ago that we dealt with a proposal for a massive on-shore windfarm at Hemswell. That plan would have blighted many of our beautiful view Park Home Energy Bills Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about the energy bill grant and park homes. Solar Farms on Agricultural Land Dear Constituent, Thank you for your recent correspondence regarding solar farms on farmland. It is the height of folly to take good productive land out of agricultural use at a time when the world’s normal food supply chains have been disrupted by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine as well as the ongoi Park Homes and Pitch Fees Dear Constituent, Thank you for your recent correspondence regarding pitch fees for park home residents. As you can imagine I have many constituents here in West Lindsey who live in park homes so they write to me fairly regularly about the various issues they are facing. My friend Sir Christopher A Level and GCSE Results Dear Constituent, Thank you for contacting me about A level and GCSE results this year. I understand this has been a distressing time for stu

pages.meta_description: Do you have a problem and want to see if Sir Edward can help? If so, please get in touch by email: edward.leigh.mp@parliament.uk

h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me | Sir Edward Leigh MP Skip to main content Sir Edward Leigh MP Member of Parliament for the Gainsborough constituency Main navigation Home News About Sir Edward Leigh About the Constituency Campaign Responses In Parliament Contact Contact Me Tweet Do you have a problem and want to see if Sir Edward can help? If so, please get in touch by email: [email protected] Or you can write by post to: Sir Edward Leigh MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA . Please be sure to include your name and postal address (so we can confirm you are a constituent) and, if possible, a telephone number or email address in case have any further questions regarding your case. If you are writing as part of a campaign or on a political issue , please check to see if he has issued a response on the subject in question. If a response has been posted, he will not normally send an individual response. Otherwise, Sir Edward will normally reply by regular post. To make a surgery appoint to see Sir Edward , please leave a voicemail with your name and telephone number with Jessie Milne, who organises Sir Edward's surgery appointments. She may be contacted by telephone on 07587147116. Note: This number is for constituents requesting a surgery appointment only . Press inquiries should not leave a message on it but contact Sir Edward via email: [email protected] Sir Edward Leigh will normally reply to correspondence from constituents via regular post. Please note, however, that a strict parliamentary convention prevents him from making any public statement on certain bills when he has chaired either a Bill Committee or the Committee of the Whole House relating to that bill. This is to ensure the impartiality of parliamentary procedure. He is also unable to take on the issues or cases of the constituents of other Members of Parliament. The office of Sir Edward Leigh MP can be contacted by telephone on 02072196480, but if you are looking for help we will need you to email or write by regular post so we have a written record of your inquiry. Email is the quickest and most efficient way for your case to be acted upon swiftly.
